decisionMakers.name: Kerry Devine
decisionMakers.note:
Could be influenced by the support the project would generate for education. 
Wants a new wastewater plant and a new school for the city 
Does not want to raise taxes. 
Could be more influenced if water is recycled 
Supportive of new forms of revenue for the city.
Influences: EDUCATION / CHAMBER
Email: kpdevine@fredericksburgva.gov

decisionMakers.name: Will Mackintosh
decisionMakers.note:
Unions can influence him. 
Water has been a major concern.
Influences: LABOR
Email: wbmackintosh@fredericksburgva.gov

decisionMakers.name: Jannan Holmes
decisionMakers.note:
Former school board member and will value educational funding. 
May follow Will McIntosh’s position.
Influences: EDUCATION
Email: jwholmes@fredericksburgva.gov

decisionMakers.name: Matt Rowe
decisionMakers.note:
Active Democrat and can be influenced by local Democrats.  
Environmentalist and also would be attracted by funding for education. 
Former school board member, like Holmes.
Influences: EDUCATION / LABOR
Email: mdrowe@fredericksburgva.gov

decisionMakers.name: Joy Crump
decisionMakers.note:
Understands business issues 
Does not want to raise taxes.
Influences: LABOR / CHAMBER
Email: jycrump@fredericksburgva.gov

decisionMakers.name: Susanna Finn
decisionMakers.note:
Workforce housing is a priority.
Her district
Influences: LABOR
Email: srfinn@fredericksburgva.gov

decisionMakers.name: Charlie Frye
decisionMakers.note:
Would be influenced by employment opportunities and funding for education. 
Member of the Teamsters Union.
Influences: LABOR
Email: clfrye@fredericksburgva.gov

risks.title: Resident quality-of-life opposition
risks.description: 48 general-opposition stakeholders are tracked in Team Copy, with recurring concerns around noise, forest removal, water, traffic, and quality of life.
risks.mitigation: Continue direct outreach, document mitigation commitments, and recruit nearby validators.

risks.title: Environmental and water advocacy
risks.description: 6 conservation stakeholders and 9 opposition groups are tracked, including Friends of the Rappahannock (FOR); Sierra Club Virginia Chapter; Rappahannock Group Sierra Club; Inform Fredericksburg.
risks.mitigation: Prepare water, environmental, and stormwater fact sheet; brief validators before public meetings.

risks.title: Social media and transparency narrative
risks.description: 2 social-media opposition stakeholders are tracked, including Inform Fredericksburg contacts.
risks.mitigation: Monitor public claims and keep council-facing answers concise, factual, and sourced.

risks.title: Site-adjacent business/access concerns
risks.description: 1 business stakeholder is categorized as opposition, with access and site-adjacent operational concerns to track.
risks.mitigation: Track access-specific concerns and route site-operation questions to the project team.

mediaActivity.note: Responded to questions asked, provided answers
